Components and Labels

*   Fuel Spoke Plenum Pressure Transducer
*   Air
*   Fuel Spoke Plenum
*   Solenoid Valve
*   Pressure Transducer
*   Accumulator Volume V
*   Flame
*   External to Pressure Vessel (2 occurrances)
*   Fuel Tee; Pressure P_tee
*   LPM Fuel Supply

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Valve modulation frequency: up to 125 Hz.
*   Air supply: 1.4 kg/s, up to 800 K, test pressures up to 30 atm.
*   Combustion zone dimensions: 19.8 cm (7.80 in) cylindrical
*   Exhaust neck dimensions: 10.5 cm i.d., 22.9 cm length
*   Combustion product quench temperature: Quenched to <475 K
*   Fuel flow modulation target: ±12% .

Here's the technical content extracted from the provided image:

1.  **Mathematical Equations:**
    * Eq (2)  (Referenced but not shown)

2.  **Tables:**

    **Table 1: Test Conditions and Observed Emissions Ratios for Figs. 5-8.**

    | Fig | Vr (no units) | Φ (no units) | control action | CO ratio | NO. ratio |
    | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
    | 5 | 69 | 0.55 | baseline | 1 | 1 |
    | 6 | 69 | 0.55 | 10 Hz | ~1 | 1.4 |
    | 7 | 59 | 0.63 | 5 Hz | ~1 | 1.16 |
    | 8 | 59 | 0.56 | 5 Hz | 42 | 2.15 |

3.  **Diagram:**

    **Fig. 4: Schematic of combustor used for single-injector test of fuel modulation**(Components and Labels)

    *   Preheated Combustion Air
    *   Sight Glass Optical Access (Three Apertures)
    *   Quench Spray Cooling Water and Air
    *   Gas Turbine Natural Gas Fuel Nozzle
    *   Plug Seal Ring
    *   Seal Ring Coolant
    *   Removable Refractory Plug
    *   Combustor Wall Cooling Water
    *   Inlet Plenum
    *   Comb. Zone
    *   Exhaust Plenum

4.  **Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

    *   Reference velocity V,
    *   reference velocities (Vr=59 m/s and 69 m/s)
    *   2.54 cm
    *   pressure = 7.8 atm
    *   two inlet air temperatures (533 K and 588 K)
    *   strong oscillation at 240 Hz
    *   OH chemiluminescence (310 nm)

title: open_loop_active_control_on_combustion_dynamics_of_gas_turbine
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 5]
Here is the technical content extracted from the provided image of the gas turbine research page:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   None present.

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Graph 1: Combustor Pressure vs. Time**
    *   X-axis: TIME (sec) from 0.0 to 0.5
    *   Y-axis: Combustor Pressure (unspecified units, likely psi) from -4 to 4
    *   Curve: Oscillating pressure signal.
*   **Graph 2: Fuel Manifold Pressure vs. Time**
    *   X-axis: TIME (sec) from 0.0 to 0.5
    *   Y-axis: Fuel Manifold Pressure (unspecified units, likely psi) from -4 to 4
    *   Curve: Oscillating pressure signal.
*   **Graph 3: Accumulator Pressure vs. Time**
    *   X-axis: TIME (sec) from 0.0 to 0.5
    *   Y-axis: Accumulator Pressure (unspecified units, likely psi) from -0.6 to 0.6
    *   Curve: Appears to be a flat and nearly constant line near 0.
*   **Graph 4: OH Emissions vs. Time**
    *   X-axis: TIME (sec) from 0.0 to 0.5
    *   Y-axis: OH Emissions (units unspecified) from 0.0 to 0.1
    *   Curve: Scattered line.
*   **Graph 5: Control Valve vs. Time**
    *   X-axis: TIME (sec) from 0.0 to 0.5
    *   Y-axis: Control Valve (UP = OPEN) from 0 to 6.
    *   Curve: Flat line at approximately 4.5.

**3. Tables:**

*  Table 1 (mentioned in Figure 5 description): Table 1 reports the ratio of NO, and CO emissions between the baseline and the modulated cases. (No data provided because the table itself is not visible in the frame.)

**4. Diagrams:**

*   Figure 9 (mentioned in text):  cross-section of the combustor, identifying the fuel injectors and eight dynamic pressure transducers (PT-1 through PT-8) (Not visible.)

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Number of premix fuel nozzles: 12
*   Number of dynamic pressure transducers: 8 (PT-1 to PT-8)
*   Pressure transducers used for fuel supply lines' dynamic effects: 2
*   Pressure transducers distributed around the perimeter of the combustor: 6 (PT 1, 2, 4, 5, 7)
*   Fiberoptic probe position to record OH chemiluminescence: 2.5 cm

title: open_loop_active_control_on_combustion_dynamics_of_gas_turbine
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 7]
Here's the technical content extracted from the provided text and image:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **X-axis:** TIME (SEC)
*   **Y-axes (Multiple):**
    *   Combustor Pressure (units: pressures in pounds per square inch from the Figure caption)
    *   Fuel Manif.  Pressure (units: pressures in pounds per square inch from the Figure caption)
    *   Accumulator Pressure (units: pressures in pounds per square inch from the Figure caption)
    *   OH Emissions
    *   Control Valve (Up = Open)

**3. Tables:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Six-overlap modulation is used.
*   Small premixed pilot flame of ~2% of the fuel is used
*   Modulation frequencies tested: 10 to 96 Hz, 24 Hz, 48 Hz, and 96 Hz.
*   Main oscillation frequency: 288 Hz
*   CO emissions rise by a factor of more than 4 for modulation frequencies <80 Hz.
*   CO increased over baseline by approximately a factor of 2 for higher modulation frequencies.

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Figure 8:** History of Signals

    *   X-axis: Time (sec), Range: 0 to 0.5
    *   Y-axes:
        *   Combustor Pressure, Range: -4 to 4
        *   Fuel Manifold Pressure, Range: -2 to 4
        *   Accumulator Pressure, Range: -0.6 to 0.4
        *   OH Emissions, Range: 0 to 0.1
        *   Control Valve (UP = OPEN), Range: 0 to 6

**3. Tables:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Figure 9:** Cross section of the engine combustor annulus

    *   Components/Labels:
        *   PT-1, PT-2, PT-3 (Fuel Manifold Side), PT-4, PT-5, PT-6 (Fuel Spokes Side), PT-7, PT-8 (Pressure Transducers). 0 Degrees
        *   OH Probe
        *   Fuel Injector (x 12)

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Fuel modulation at 40 Hz
*   Pressure transducers (PT) located 60 deg apart on the liner.
*   Duty cycles of <50%
*   Six solenoids overlap, six solenoids open at any time.

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Axes:**
    *   Y-axis: Normalized Emission or RMS (unitless)
    *   X-axis: Modulation Frequency (unspecified units implying Hz)
*   **Curves:**
    *   RMS (% of combustor pressure)
    *   Norm NOx
    *   Norm CO
*   **Key Values:**
    *   Base frequency: RMS approximately 0.8, NOx approximately 0.2, CO approximately 0.1
    *   At 10 Hz: RMS approximately 5, NOx approximately 0.9, CO approximately 0.6
    *   At 96 Hz: RMS approximately 3, NOx approximately 0.8, CO approximately 0.1

**3. Tables:**

    *   Gain Voltage Supply

    *   110 VAC
    *   OH PMT
    *   Fiber
    *   "Driver Box"
    *   Solenoid Wires
    *   OH Probe (indicated by triangle)
    *   Kistler (indicated by square)
    *   Solenoid (indicated by circle)
*   **Components and Labels (Control Room):**
    *   Printer
    *   OH PMT Out BNC
    *   Kistler Lead Wires
    *   TEAC/PC Cable
    *   TEAC DATA Recorder
    *   Driver Power Cables
    *   Charge Couplers/ Cbles as needed for Press. & PWM
    *   12 BNC Control Cables
    *   TEE Connect as Needed
    *   L Zonic FFT Analyzer
    *   110 VAC
    *   Control Computer

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   110 VAC (voltage supply)
*   12 Solenoid wire pairs
*   6+2 Kistler lead wires
*   Normalized CO value of 1.3 at highest modulation frequency compared to baseline
*   Fourfold increase in CO from activating one solenoid

text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 10]
Here's the technical content extracted from the provided image:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Figure 12 (a)**: Dynamics when Solenoid triggers are not active
    *   **Axes:**
        *   PT1 PRESSURE (Pa) vs Time (sec)
        *   PT2 PRESSURE (Pa) vs Time (sec)
        *   OH SIGNAL (V) vs Time (sec)
        *   SOLENOID 2 TRIGGER (v) vs Time (sec)
        *   SOLENOID 3 TRIGGER (v) vs Time (sec)
    *    Time from 0.00 to 0.10 seconds.

*   **Figure 12 (b)**: Observed dynamics with odd-even fuel modulation activated at 40Hz.
    *   **Axes:**
        *   PT1 PRESSURE (Pa) vs Time (sec)
        *   PT2 PRESSURE (Pa) vs Time (sec)
        *   OH SIGNAL (V) vs Time (sec)
        *   SOLENOID 2 TRIGGER (v) vs Time (sec)
        *   SOLENOID 3 TRIGGER (v) vs Time (sec)
*    Time from 0.00 to 0.10 seconds.
*   **Figure 13:** Rms Pressure and Emissions
    *   **Axes:**
        *   x-axis: "Odd-number injectors open" (Base, #1, #1-3, #1-5, #1-7, #1-9, #1-11)
        *   y-axis: "Normalized Emission or RMS" (values from 0 to 5)
    *   **Curves:**
        *   RMS (%)
        *   Norm NOx
        *   Norm CO
*   **Figure 14:** Effect of Fuel Modulation on Pollutant Emissions
    *   **Axes:**
        *   x-axis: "Modulation Frequency" (Base, 40, 60, 80, 100, 125)
        *   y-axis: "Normalized Emission or RMS" (values from 0 to 5)
    *   **Curves:**
        *   RMS (%)
        *   Norm NOx
        *   Norm CO

**3. Tables:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Fuel Modulation Frequency: 40 Hz (in Fig. 12(b))
*   Odd-number injectors (i.e., injectors 1-7=1, 3, 5, 7, etc.)
